name: Barbell Bench Press - Medium Grip
instructions: Setup: Grip slightly wider than shoulder-width; arch upper back, feet flat; retract and depress scapulae.
Execution: Lower bar to lower chest with elbows ~60° from torso; press in a slight arc back up.
Common error: Elbows flaring to 90° — increases shoulder impingement risk; tuck them slightly.
Common error: Bouncing off chest — control the eccentric; brief pause at bottom.

name: Barbell Curls Lying Against An Incline
Barbell Deadlift
instructions: Setup: Bar over mid-foot, hip-width stance; hinge to grip just outside legs; neutral spine.
Execution: Push the floor away, drag bar up shins; once past knees, drive hips forward to lockout.
Common error: Lower-back rounding — brace harder and hinge rather than squat the pull.
Common error: Bar drifting forward — keep it close to body throughout the lift.

name: Barbell Full Squat
instructions: Setup: Bar on upper traps; feet shoulder-width, toes slightly out; brace core before unracking.
Execution: Break at hips and knees simultaneously; descend until thighs are parallel or below.
Drive: Push through full foot back to standing; keep chest up throughout.
Common error: Knees caving inward (valgus) — actively push knees out over toes.

name: Barbell Hip Thrust
instructions: Setup: Upper back on bench edge; padded bar across hips; feet flat, hip-width, close to body.
Execution: Drive hips up to full extension (body parallel to floor); squeeze glutes hard at the top.
Common error: Overextending the lower back — keep ribs down and pelvis neutral at lockout.
Common error: Feet too far away — reduces glute activation; bring feet closer to your hips.

name: Barbell Incline Bench Press - Medium Grip
instructions: Setup: Bench at 30–45°; grip same as flat bench; retract shoulder blades against the pad.
Execution: Lower to upper chest; press up and slightly back to the starting position.
Common error: Bench angle above 45° — shifts load from upper chest to front deltoids.
Common error: Wrists bent back — stack wrists over elbows and keep them neutral.

name: Bent Over Barbell Row
instructions: Setup: Hip-hinge to ~45° torso angle; overhand grip wider than shoulder-width; neutral spine.
Execution: Drive elbows back and up to lower sternum; squeeze shoulder blades at top.
Common error: Using hip momentum — initiate the pull with your back, not a lower-body thrust.
Common error: Rounding the back — engage lats before starting the pull.

name: Bent Over Dumbbell Rear Delt Raise With Head On Bench
Bent Over One-Arm Long Bar Row
Bent Over Two-Arm Long Bar Row
Bent Over Two-Dumbbell Row With Palms In
Bosu Ball Cable Crunch With Side Bends
Bottoms-Up Clean From The Hang Position
Calf Press On The Leg Press Machine
Chest And Front Of Shoulder Stretch
Close-Grip Barbell Bench Press
instructions: Setup: Shoulder-width grip; lie flat and retract scapulae; elbows tucked close to torso.
Execution: Lower bar to lower chest; press focusing on triceps extension; elbows stay in throughout.
Common error: Grip too narrow — causes wrist strain; shoulder-width is sufficient.
Common error: Elbows flaring out — defeats triceps focus; keep them at ~30° from torso.

name: Close-Grip Push-Up off of a Dumbbell
Decline Close-Grip Bench To Skull Crusher
Dips - Triceps Version
instructions: Setup: Grip parallel bars, arms straight; stay upright (lean forward to shift load to chest).
Execution: Lower until elbows reach 90°; press back to full extension without locking out aggressively.
Common error: Dropping too deep — strains shoulders; stop at 90° elbow bend.
Common error: Elbows flaring wide — keep them tucked close to the torso.

name: Dumbbell Bench Press with Neutral Grip
Dumbbell Lying One-Arm Rear Lateral Raise
Extended Range One-Arm Kettlebell Floor Press
Face Pull
instructions: Setup: Cable at face height with rope attachment; step back far enough for full extension.
Execution: Pull rope to either side of face with elbows high and flared; externally rotate at peak.
Common error: Elbows dropping below shoulder height — shifts load to biceps, not rear delts.
Common error: Too much weight — use a light load and focus on the contraction and external rotation.

name: Front Barbell Squat To A Bench
Front Squat (Clean Grip)
instructions: Setup: Bar on front deltoids; elbows high with upper arms parallel to floor; feet shoulder-width.
Execution: Squat with upright torso; keep elbows high throughout to prevent bar rolling off shoulders.
Common error: Elbows dropping — causes bar to roll; build wrist and thoracic flexibility.
Common error: Forward lean — front squat demands more upper-back and ankle mobility than back squat.

name: Hammer Grip Incline DB Bench Press
Incline Dumbbell Bench With Palms Facing In
Incline Dumbbell Flyes - With A Twist
Intermediate Hip Flexor and Quad Stretch
Isometric Neck Exercise - Front And Back
Kneeling Cable Crunch With Alternating Oblique Twists
Leg Extensions
instructions: Setup: Back against pad; knee at the joint edge; align machine axis with your knee joint.
Execution: Extend to near full lockout; hold 1 second; lower slowly over 3 seconds.
Common error: Rushing the eccentric — the lowering phase builds quad strength; control it.
Common error: Seat too far back — shifts shear force onto the knee; adjust the seat forward.

name: Leg Press
instructions: Setup: Feet shoulder-width, mid-platform; toes slightly out; lower back flat against pad.
Execution: Lower sled until knees reach 90°; press through full foot; avoid locking knees at top.
Common error: Knees caving inward — push them out in line with toes throughout.
Common error: Hips rising off pad — reduces ROM and loads the lower back.

name: Lying Close-Grip Bar Curl On High Pulley
Lying Close-Grip Barbell Triceps Extension Behind The Head
Lying Close-Grip Barbell Triceps Press To Chin
Lying Face Down Plate Neck Resistance
Lying Face Up Plate Neck Resistance
Lying Leg Curls
instructions: Setup: Lie face down; pad rests just above heels; hips flat on the bench.
Execution: Curl heels toward glutes through full range; brief hold at top; lower slowly.
Common error: Hips rising off the pad — reduces hamstring isolation; keep them down.
Common error: Short range — aim for full contraction then full extension every rep.

name: One-Arm Kettlebell Military Press To The Side
One Arm Pronated Dumbbell Triceps Extension
One Arm Supinated Dumbbell Triceps Extension
Palms-Down Dumbbell Wrist Curl Over A Bench
Palms-Down Wrist Curl Over A Bench
Palms-Up Barbell Wrist Curl Over A Bench
Palms-Up Dumbbell Wrist Curl Over A Bench
Push-Ups With Feet On An Exercise Ball
Romanian Deadlift
instructions: Setup: Start standing with bar at hips; push hips back with a slight knee bend to hinge forward.
Execution: Lower bar along shins until hamstring stretch; drive hips forward to return to standing.
Common error: Excessive knee bend — this becomes a conventional deadlift; keep knees soft and stable.
Common error: Lower-back rounding — stop when your back rounds; hamstring flexibility is the limit.

name: Seated Bent-Over One-Arm Dumbbell Triceps Extension
Seated Bent-Over Two-Arm Dumbbell Triceps Extension
Seated Cable Rows
instructions: Setup: Cable at low position; sit with soft knee bend; grip handles with back upright.
Execution: Drive elbows back to ribcage, squeezing shoulder blades; control the return to full stretch.
Common error: Torso swinging — initiate with elbows and back, not a lower-body lean.
Common error: No stretch at the front — allow a slight forward lean with scapular protraction.

name: Seated One-Arm Dumbbell Palms-Down Wrist Curl
Seated One-Arm Dumbbell Palms-Up Wrist Curl
Seated Two-Arm Palms-Up Low-Pulley Wrist Curl
Smith Machine Behind the Back Shrug
Standing Bent-Over One-Arm Dumbbell Triceps Extension
Standing Bent-Over Two-Arm Dumbbell Triceps Extension
Standing Dumbbell Straight-Arm Front Delt Raise Above Head
Standing Front Barbell Raise Over Head
Standing One-Arm Dumbbell Curl Over Incline Bench
Standing Palms-Up Barbell Behind The Back Wrist Curl
Sumo Deadlift
instructions: Setup: Wide stance, toes 30–45° out; grip bar inside legs; hips low, neutral spine, chest up.
Execution: Push knees out over toes and press floor away; lock out hips and knees simultaneously.
Common error: Knees collapsing inward — drive them out aggressively from the floor.
Common error: Hips rising too fast — maintain hip angle off the floor until bar clears knees.

name: Overhead Press
instructions: Setup: Barbell at collarbone height; grip just outside shoulders; brace core and squeeze glutes.
Execution: Press vertically, tuck chin back as bar passes face; lock out overhead with ears between arms.
Common error: Excessive lower-back arch — engage glutes and abs to stay neutral.
Common error: Bar drifting forward — aim straight up, not out in front.

name: Arnold Press
instructions: Setup: Dumbbells at shoulder level, palms facing you; sit upright with core braced.
Execution: As you press up, rotate palms to face forward by full extension; reverse the rotation on descent.
Common error: Rushing the rotation — the rotation IS the exercise; control it through the full ROM.
Common error: Leaning back excessively — keep back flat and avoid using momentum.

name: Lat Pulldown
instructions: Setup: Wide overhand grip; thighs secured under pads; slight backward lean (~15°).
Execution: Pull bar to upper chest driving elbows toward hips; return slowly to full arm extension.
Common error: Pulling behind the neck — increases cervical risk; always pull to the front.
Common error: Using momentum — control the weight; slow the eccentric phase.

name: Pull-Up
instructions: Setup: Overhand grip slightly wider than shoulders; full hang with shoulders active (not shrugged).
Execution: Drive elbows toward hips to pull chin above bar; lower slowly over 2–3 seconds.
Common error: Kipping or swinging — strict form builds far more strength.
Common error: Partial range — start from a dead hang each rep for full muscle recruitment.
